proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with lower probability of objective_response in this AML cohort.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic regression: each 1-point increase in ecog_ps lowers log-odds of response by -0.384 (p=1.84e-97). Response rate falls from 21.3% at ECOG 0 to 15.5% at ECOG 1 to 11.2% at ECOG 2.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Female sex (sex_female=1) is associated with a different probability of objective_response than male sex.

analyses.result_summary: Age is essentially flat against response: log-OR per year = 0.00040 (p=0.718). Mean age is 67.98 in non-responders vs 68.03 in responders.
No sex difference in response: log-OR for female = 0.024 (p=0.320).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher symptom burden (sum of fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, appetite_loss_grade) is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Each 1% increase in 6-month weight loss lowers log-odds of response by 0.0401 (coef=-0.0401, p=7.43e-35). Mean weight loss 3.95% in non-responders vs 3.38% in responders.
The composite symptom-burden score is not associated with response (coef=-0.0013, p=0.707). Individual grades were also weak (only appetite_loss_grade reached p<0.05).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher blast_pct_marrow at diagnosis is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l (systemic inflammation) is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Each 1-point higher marrow blast % lowers log-odds of response by 0.0057 (coef=-0.0057, p=1.16e-19). Mean blast % 60.3 vs 58.2 in non-responders vs responders.
Higher WBC at diagnosis is associated with lower response (coef=-0.00216, p=1.88e-05).
Higher CRP is associated with lower response (coef=-0.00700, p=2.82e-06). Mean CRP 6.17 in non-responders vs 5.65 in responders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl (better nutritional/host status) is associated with higher objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Each 1 g/dL higher albumin raises log-odds of response by 0.115 (p=1.36e-06). Mean 3.80 in non-responders vs 3.82 in responders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: complex_karyotype is associated with lower objective_response (main effect, marginal over treatment).
tp53_mutation is associated with lower objective_response (main effect, marginal over treatment).
secondary_aml is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Marginal main effect of complex_karyotype on response is null (coef=-0.0083, p=0.805); response rate 16.8% with vs 16.9% without.
No marginal effect of tp53_mutation on response (coef=-0.0266, p=0.507); response 16.6% with vs 16.9% without.
No effect of secondary_aml on response (coef=-0.0033, p=0.904).

proposed_hypotheses.text: idh1_mutation is associated with HIGHER objective_response (positive marginal effect).
idh2_mutation is associated with higher objective_response (mirror hypothesis).
npm1_mutation is associated with higher objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: IDH1 mutation strongly associated with higher response: coef=+0.364, p=4.13e-18. Response rate 22.1% in IDH1+ vs 16.5% in IDH1- (chi2 p=3.52e-18).
IDH2 has no marginal effect on response (coef=-0.0287, p=0.488). The IDH1 finding does not generalize to IDH2.
NPM1 trends toward higher response but is not significant marginally (coef=0.0418, p=0.106). Response 17.3% in NPM1+ vs 16.7% in NPM1-.

proposed_hypotheses.text: flt3_itd is associated with a different objective_response than wildtype.
flt3_tkd is associated with a different objective_response than wildtype.

analyses.result_summary: No marginal effect of FLT3-ITD on response (coef=0.0253, p=0.394).
No marginal effect of FLT3-TKD on response (coef=-0.0485, p=0.356).

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with higher objective_response than non-receipt.
treatment_7plus3 is associated with higher objective_response than non-receipt.
FLT3 inhibitors (treatment_midostaurin or treatment_gilteritinib) have a positive main effect on objective_response unconditional on FLT3 status.
IDH inhibitors (treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib) have a positive main effect on objective_response unconditional on IDH status.

analyses.result_summary: Ven/aza has a small positive marginal effect: coef=0.0525, p=0.031; response 17.3% on vs 16.6% off.
No marginal effect of 7+3 on response (coef=0.0150, p=0.532); response 17.0% on vs 16.8% off.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_midostaurin', data=df).fit() and same for gilteritinib
analyses.result_summary: Midostaurin null (coef=-0.0038, p=0.910); gilteritinib non-significant (coef=-0.0511, p=0.130). Neither has a positive marginal effect.

analyses.code: same logistic regressions for ivosidenib and enasidenib
analyses.result_summary: Ivosidenib null (coef=0.0143, p=0.746); enasidenib non-significant negative (coef=-0.0623, p=0.166). Neither has a positive marginal effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In flt3_itd-positive patients, treatment_midostaurin produces a higher objective_response than non-receipt of midostaurin (positive flt3_itd × treatment_midostaurin interaction).
In flt3_itd-positive patients, treatment_gilteritinib produces a higher objective_response than non-receipt of gilteritinib (positive flt3_itd × treatment_gilteritinib interaction).
In flt3_tkd-positive patients, treatment_gilteritinib produces a higher objective_response than non-receipt of gilteritinib.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction is negative and not significant (coef=-0.105, p=0.219). Within FLT3-ITD+ subgroup, midostaurin coef=-0.088 (p=0.250). No detectable benefit.
Interaction not significant (coef=0.034, p=0.686). Within FLT3-ITD+, gilteritinib coef=-0.024 (p=0.749). No detectable benefit.
Interaction not significant (coef=-0.152, p=0.327). No detectable benefit of gilteritinib in FLT3-TKD+ subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In idh1_mutation-positive patients, treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response (positive idh1_mutation × treatment_ivosidenib interaction).
In idh2_mutation-positive patients, treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response (positive idh2_mutation × treatment_enasidenib interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction is significantly NEGATIVE (coef=-0.364, p=0.027). Within IDH1+, ivosidenib actually lowers response (coef=-0.320, p=0.043). The hypothesized positive interaction is REFUTED; the direction is opposite.
Interaction not significant (coef=-0.124, p=0.448). Within IDH2+, enasidenib coef=-0.176 (p=0.261). No benefit detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: npm1_mutation-positive patients receive a larger boost from treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine than NPM1- patients (positive npm1_mutation × treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Strong positive interaction: coef=0.261, p=6.65e-07. Within NPM1+, ven/aza coef=+0.233 (p=9.02e-08); response rate 19.3% with vs 16.0% without ven/aza in NPM1+. In NPM1-, no benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In tp53_mutation-positive patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces a smaller (or no) objective_response benefit compared with TP53 wild-type (negative tp53_mutation × treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine interaction).
In tp53_mutation-positive patients, treatment_7plus3 produces a smaller objective_response benefit compared with TP53 wild-type.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction not significant (coef=-0.087, p=0.285); the directional sign is negative (less benefit in TP53+) but the data do not support a robust effect.
Interaction not significant (coef=-0.044, p=0.588). No measurable TP53-by-7+3 interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In complex_karyotype-positive patients, treatment_7plus3 yields a lower objective_response than in CK-negative patients (negative complex_karyotype × treatment_7plus3 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Significant negative interaction: coef=-0.213, p=0.0016. Within CK+, 7+3 lowers response (coef=-0.167, p=0.008); in CK+ patients receiving 7+3 the response rate was 15.5% vs 17.9% in CK+ patients not on 7+3.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In unfit_for_intensive-positive patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces a larger objective_response benefit than in fit patients (positive unfit_for_intensive × treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine interaction).
In unfit_for_intensive-positive patients, treatment_7plus3 yields a different objective_response than in fit patients.

analyses.result_summary: Significant positive interaction: coef=+0.104, p=0.036. Within unfit, ven/aza raises log-odds of response by 0.114 (p=0.0027); response 17.9% on vs 16.3% off.
Interaction not significant (coef=-0.033, p=0.502); within unfit, 7+3 has no effect on response (coef=-0.0046, p=0.902).

proposed_hypotheses.text: atrial_fibrillation is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Atrial fibrillation lowers response: coef=-0.101, p=0.024. Response 15.6% with afib vs 17.0% without (chi2 p=0.025).

proposed_hypotheses.text: snp_rs1050828 (a known G6PD variant) is associated with a different objective_response than non-carriers.
At least one of the 24 pharmacogenomic snp_* variants is significantly associated with objective_response (multiplicity-uncorrected).

analyses.result_summary: snp_rs1050828 is associated with higher response (coef=+0.118, p=0.0053).

analyses.code: loop logistic over all 24 snp_* columns
analyses.result_summary: Across 24 SNPs, only 2 reach uncorrected p<0.05 (rs1050828 p=0.005, rs2032582 p=0.049). After Bonferroni (alpha=0.05/24=0.0021), only rs1050828 survives. Most pharmacogenomic SNPs show no signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: objective_response varies across race_ethnicity categories.
objective_response varies across insurance_type categories.
rural_residence is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Modest overall heterogeneity by race_ethnicity (LRT p=0.009); response rates white 17.3%, hispanic 16.7%, asian 16.3%, black 15.7%, other 14.7%. White patients show the highest response, 'other' the lowest.
No heterogeneity by insurance_type (LRT p=0.540); response 16.6-17.2% across all categories.
No effect of rural residence on response (coef=0.00025, p=0.993).

proposed_hypotheses.text: More prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with lower objective_response.
prior_chemotherapy is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: No effect of prior lines on response (coef=0.0068, p=0.515).
No effect of prior chemotherapy on response (coef=-0.0130, p=0.588).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher total_bilirubin_mg_dl is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher creatinine_mg_dl is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: No effect of bilirubin on response (coef=-0.0500, p=0.188).
No effect of creatinine on response (coef=-0.0215, p=0.592).
No effect of LDH on response (coef=-0.000138, p=0.351).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A composite high-risk feature (tp53_mutation OR complex_karyotype) is associated with lower objective_response, even though neither showed an effect alone.
The high-risk composite modifies the effect of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Composite high-risk has no marginal effect on response (coef=-0.0137, p=0.627). Combining the two adverse cytogenetic markers does not produce a stronger univariate signal.
Interaction not significant (coef=-0.0082, p=0.887). Ven/aza benefit does not differ between high-risk and other patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive idh1_mutation main effect on objective_response is at least partly attributable to a synergistic idh1_mutation × treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine interaction (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Strong positive interaction: coef=+0.730, p=7.73e-18. IDH1+ patients on ven/aza enjoy a much larger response boost than IDH1+ patients off ven/aza, supporting that the IDH1 'main effect' really reflects synergy with venetoclax-based therapy rather than an intrinsic disease feature.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine benefit on objective_response varies with ecog_ps (interaction).
The treatment_7plus3 effect on objective_response varies with age_years (interaction).
The atrial_fibrillation effect on objective_response varies with age_years (interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction not significant (coef=-0.026, p=0.483); ven/aza benefit does not vary meaningfully across ECOG strata.
Interaction not significant (coef=0.00112, p=0.612); 7+3 effect is flat across age.
Interaction not significant (coef=0.0048, p=0.247); atrial fibrillation effect on response does not vary with age.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic regression for objective_response, ecog_ps, weight_loss_pct_6mo, blast_pct_marrow, crp_mg_l, wbc_k_per_ul, idh1_mutation, albumin_g_dl, atrial_fibrillation, and treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine remain independent predictors after mutual adjustment.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ age_years + ecog_ps + weight_loss_pct_6mo + blast_pct_marrow + albumin_g_dl + crp_mg_l + wbc_k_per_ul + idh1_mutation + idh2_mutation + tp53_mutation + complex_karyotype + npm1_mutation + flt3_itd + secondary_aml + treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine + treatment_7plus3 + treatment_midostaurin + treatment_gilteritinib + treatment_ivosidenib + treatment_enasidenib + atrial_fibrillation + sex_female', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In the multivariable model, the independently significant predictors are: ecog_ps (coef=-0.383, p=1.38e-96), weight_loss_pct_6mo (coef=-0.040, p=1.10e-34), blast_pct_marrow (coef=-0.0058, p=5.33e-20), albumin_g_dl (coef=+0.118, p=1.02e-06), crp_mg_l (coef=-0.0072, p=1.60e-06), wbc_k_per_ul (coef=-0.0022, p=1.09e-05), idh1_mutation (coef=+0.359, p=2.93e-17), atrial_fibrillation (coef=-0.089, p=0.049), and treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine (coef=+0.059, p=0.016). Age, sex, FLT3-ITD, IDH2, TP53, NPM1, complex_karyotype, secondary_aml, 7+3, midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, and enasidenib were NOT independent predictors. Hypothesis is supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strongest treatment-biomarker signal in this cohort is npm1_mutation × treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine (positive synergy), with a secondary positive idh1_mutation × treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine synergy and a negative complex_karyotype × treatment_7plus3 interaction; targeted FLT3 and IDH inhibitors do not show their expected biomarker-matched response benefits.

analyses.result_summary: Confirmed across iterations: npm1_mutation × ven/aza interaction coef=+0.261 (p=6.65e-07); idh1_mutation × ven/aza interaction coef=+0.730 (p=7.73e-18); complex_karyotype × 7+3 interaction coef=-0.213 (p=0.0016); unfit × ven/aza interaction coef=+0.104 (p=0.036). FLT3-ITD/TKD × midostaurin and × gilteritinib interactions are NOT significant; IDH2 × enasidenib interaction is NOT significant; idh1_mutation × ivosidenib interaction is significantly NEGATIVE in this cohort, contrary to clinical expectation. Hypothesis supported.
